REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 19, 2021. BY NO LATER THAN MARCH 12, 2021 PLAINTIFF TO FILE A PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT ALLEGES CAUSES OF ACTION IN CLEAR AND CONCISE LANGUAGE WITH FACTS SUPPORTING EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. EACH CAUSE OF ACTION SHALL CLEARLY INDICATE THE DEFENDANT AGAINST WHOM THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS ASSERTED. PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT SHALL COMPLY WITH ALL PRIOR ORDERS IN THIS CASE INCLUDING THE ORDER ON MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, DISCOVERY ORDERS AND PRIOR ORDERS ON MOTIONS FOR LEAVE TO AMEND. WHILE NOT REQUIRED, THE COURT SUGGESTS THAT THE PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT INCLUDE RELEVANT FACTS WITHIN EACH CAUSE OF ACTION (AS OPPOSED TO STATING ALL FACTS IN THE "GENERAL" SECTION AND INCORPORATING THOSE FACTS INTO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION). THIS ORGANIZATION WILL PROMOTE EFFICIENCY AND WILL ENSURE THAT RELEVANT FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE PROPERLY ASSERTED AVOIDING FUTURE UNNECESSARY MOTION PRACTICE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(501/CFH)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ON FEBRUARY 10, 2021 PLAINTIFF'S EX-PARTE APPLICATION TO CALENDAR A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND WAS GRANTED; AND THE MOTION WAS CALENDARED FOR FEBRUARY 16, 2021. PER FEBRUARY 16, 2021 TENTATIVE RULING/MINUTE ORDER THE MOTION WAS CONTINUED AS FOLLOWS: "PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 19, 2021. BY NO LATER THAN MARCH 12, 2021 PLAINTIFF TO FILE A PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT ALLEGES CAUSES OF ACTION IN CLEAR AND CONCISE LANGUAGE WITH FACTS SUPPORTING EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. EACH CAUSE OF ACTION SHALL CLEARLY INDICATE THE DEFENDANT AGAINST WHOM THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS ASSERTED. PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT SHALL COMPLY WITH ALL PRIOR ORDERS IN THIS CASE INCLUDING THE ORDER ON MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, DISCOVERY ORDERS AND PRIOR ORDERS ON MOTIONS FOR LEAVE TO AMEND. WHILE NOT REQUIRED, THE COURT SUGGESTS THAT THE PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT INCLUDE RELEVANT FACTS WITHIN EACH CAUSE OF ACTION (AS OPPOSED TO STATING ALL FACTS IN THE "GENERAL" SECTION AND INCORPORATING THOSE FACTS INTO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION). THIS ORGANIZATION WILL PROMOTE EFFICIENCY AND WILL ENSURE THAT RELEVANT FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE PROPERLY ASSERTED AVOIDING FUTURE UNNECESSARY MOTION PRACTICE." PLAINTIFF FAILED TO MAKE THE REQUIRED FILINGS AND FAILED TO APPEAR ON MARCH 19, 2021. AS OF MARCH 19, 2021, MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT MA HAS ALREADY BEEN GRANTED. ON APRIL 1, 2021 PLAINTIFF FILED THIS MOTION. ATTACHED TO THE MOTION IS A PROPOSED 84-PAGE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT STILL NAMES MA AS A DEFENDANT AND DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE FEBRUARY 16, 2021 MINUTE ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUN-03-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON'S MOTION FOR REQUEST FOR RECONSIDERATION OF NEW EVIDENCE UNDER CCP 1008, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION DENIED. PER CCP 1008(A), THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF VARIOUS PRIOR DISCOVERY ORDERS IS UNTIMELY. THE LAST ORDER REFERENCED BY PLAINTIFF WAS A MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS. THE ORDER ON THAT MOTION WAS SERVED BY THE COURT ON MARCH 22, 2021. ANY MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION WAS DUE ON APRIL 6, 2021 (10 DAYS + 5 DAYS FOR MAIL). THIS MOTION WAS FILED ON APRIL 7, 2021. THE OTHER ORDERS REFERENCED BY PLAINTIFF WERE ISSUED WEEKS OR MONTHS BEFORE APRIL 7, 2021. MOREOVER, EVEN IF THE MOTIONS WERE TIMELY, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW AS REQUIRED BY CCP 1008(A). MOTION / Notice Of & Motion Of Ccp 473(B)For Discretionary Relief Due To A Excusable Mistake, Surprise, Inadvertence To Vacate April 23, 2021 Order For Costs, Fees, And Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON'S NOTICE OF & MOTION OF CCP 473(B) FOR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF DUE TO EXCUSABLE MISTAKE, SURPRISE, INADVERTENCE TO VACATE APRIL 23, 2021 ORDER FOR COSTS, FEES, AND SANCTIONS is DENIED. There is no April 23, 2021 Order for Costs, Fess and Sanctions on file. Register of Actions does not reflect any orders entered on that day. On April 23, 2021 the Court heard and DENIED Defendant Ma's Motion for Attorney's Fees and the prevailing party (here Plaintiff) was instructed to provide a proposed order for execution. MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON's Motion For Leave To File Proposed First Amended Complaint is DENIED. On February 10, 2021 Plaintiff's Ex-parte Application to calendar a Motion for Leave to Amend was granted; and the motion was calendared for February 16, 2021. Per February 16, 2021 tentative ruling/minute order the motion was continued as follows: "Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to Amend Complaint is continued to March 19, 2021. By no later than March 12, 2021 Plaintiff to file a proposed amended complaint that alleges causes of action in clear and concise language with facts supporting each element of each cause of action. Each cause of action shall clearly indicate the defendant against whom the cause of action is asserted. Proposed amended complaint shall comply with all prior orders in this case including the order on Motions for Summary Adjudication, Discovery Orders and prior orders on motions for leave to amend. While not required, the Court suggests that the proposed amended complaint include relevant facts within each cause of action (as opposed to stating all facts in the "General" section and incorporating those facts into each cause of action). This organization will promote efficiency and will ensure that relevant facts in support of each element of each cause of action are properly asserted avoiding future unnecessary motion practice."
Plaintiff failed to make the required filings and failed to appear on March 19, 2021. As of March 19, 2021, motion for terminating sanctions filed by defendant Ma has already been granted. On April 1, 2021 Plaintiff filed this motion. Attached to the motion is a proposed 84-page First Amended Complaint that still names Ma as a defendant and does not comply with the February 16, 2021 minute order. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
